rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY DUNCAN VS. GEARY COURTYARD ASSOCIATES ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 18, 2024 line 2. DEFENDANT EQUITY RESIDENTIAL MANAGEMENT, L.L.C. (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AND SERVED HEREIN AS EQUITY RESIDENTIAL), MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is GRANTED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts supporting punitive damages against a corporate defendant. =(501/JMQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: DCO MISSION BAY LP, A DELAWARE LIMITED PARTNERSHIP VS. CHER CLARK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Stipulation; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 18, 2024 line 4. PLAINTIFF DCO MISSION BAY LP, A DELAWARE LIMITED PARTNERSHIP MOTION FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO STIPULATION; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES is DENIED without prejudice. Plaintiff to immediately restore Defendant's access to the online payment portal available to tenants. Plaintiff shall notify the defendant when the portal is functional and available for Defendant to use. Plaintiff shall not re-file the motion unless Plaintiff confirms that the portal is functioning and available to defendant to use; and the defendant was provided reasonable notice and opportunity to pay via portal. =(501/JMQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MARIE NARLOCK VS. WALID JADULLAH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion For Enforcement Of Settlement Agreement, And Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Ccp 664.6
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 18, 2024 line 8. PLAINTIFF MARIE NARLOCK , TRUSTEE OF THE RICHARD A. NARLOCK & MARIE L. NARLOCK FAMILY TRUST NOTICE AND MOTION FOR ENFORCEMENT OF SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT, AND ENTRY OF JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO CCP 664.6 is DENIED. No admissible evidence provided that Defendant is in possession of the premises. No evidence provided that Plaintiff fulfilled their obligations re: mold inspection and remediation at the premises per paragraph 4(w) to now seek enforcement of the defendant's payment obligation for the use of the premises where Plaintiff's habitability obligations are not only dictated by contract, but also by strong public policy. =(501/JMQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
